meaning: n.[亦作glamor] 魔力,魅力v.迷惑
examples: We need someone with youth, glamour and pizzazz.
Being a model invests her with a certain glamour.
Royce developed his disenchantment with glamour stocks and his preference for oddballs in the early 1970s.
If tradition holds, even more patent litigation will follow--patent law has become a legal glamour job.
